label: 101_pakistan_pakistans_pakistani_afghanistan
description: The U.S. has suspended military aid to Pakistan due to its support for the Taliban and Haqqani network, while Pakistan seeks to redefine its relationship beyond Afghanistan as the U.S. withdraws from the region.

earliest_articles.title: U.S.-Pakistan Strategic Dialogue in Doha, Qatar
earliest_articles.excerpt: INTRODUCTION On February 15-16, 2010, Brookings (Steve Cohen and myself) organized a conference on U.S.-Pakistan strategic relations in Doha, supported by the Near East and South Asia Center at National Defense University, as well as the Government of Qatar. The Pakistani participants for the meeting included a range…

earliest_articles.title: Pakistan Trip Report: Islamabad, Peshawar and Points in Between
earliest_articles.excerpt: In mid-March, at the invitation of the Pakistani Embassy in Washington and the country’s Inter-Services Intelligence Directorate, or ISI, I traveled to Islamabad, Peshawar, and points in between. The Pakistanis invited me in February and encouraged me to go quickly—partly because the weather is better in March and…

newest_articles.title: Pakistan’s democracy, its military, and America
newest_articles.excerpt: Pakistan has a new government in power. The country held a delayed, flawed election on February 8, 2024, marked by a pre-poll crackdown on its most popular political party, the Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI), arrests of thousands of PTI members and senior leadership, and knocking the party name and election symbol off…

newest_articles.title: The Biden administration’s two-track Pakistan policy misses the mark
newest_articles.excerpt: The U.S.-Pakistan relationship has weathered several bumps in the road over the past two years, including, most prominently, the fallout from the Afghanistan withdrawal and the Taliban takeover. The Biden administration has now settled on a bureaucratic division of labor in its policy toward Pakistan: a lack of…

central_articles.title: An uneasy limbo for US-Pakistan relations amidst the withdrawal from Afghanistan
central_articles.excerpt: Six months into the Biden administration, amid the U.S. withdrawal from Afghanistan and increasing violence on the ground there, the U.S.-Pakistan relationship stands in uneasy limbo. Pakistan has indicated repeatedly that it wants the relationship to be defined more broadly than with regard to Afghanistan —…

central_articles.title: Why Pakistan supports terrorist groups, and why the US finds it so hard to induce change
central_articles.excerpt: The Trump administration’s decision to suspend military aid to Pakistan is one of the most significant U.S. punitive actions against Pakistan since 2001. The United States has long been frustrated with Pakistan’s persistent acquiescence to safe havens for the Afghan Taliban and its vicious Haqqani branch in Pakistan…
